title: leucanthemum oppositifolium
intro: Leucanthemum oppositifolium, also known as the opposite-leaved daisy or Pyrenean daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Pyrenees and other mountainous regions in southern Europe. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum oppositifolium include its ability to reduce fever and treat respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Leucanthemum oppositifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
